### Which area(s) of Next.js are affected? (leave empty if unsure)

App directory (appDir: true)

### Link to the code that reproduces this issue

https://github.com/myl7/next-ignore-dynamic-params-mre

### To Reproduce

The MRE repo contains the code to reproduce the error.

- `pnpm run build && pnpm run start`
- `curl -I http://localhost:3000/posts/1`, returns 200 correctly
- `curl -I http://localhost:3000/posts/2`, return 200 wrongly, expecting 404

### Describe the Bug

When using `generateStaticParams` with `next/headers`, Next.js will return an unexpected error: `Error: Dynamic server usage: headers`. The error can be avoided by disable either `generateStaticParams` or `next/headers` in the development mode. Related https://github.com/vercel/next.js/issues/43427 https://github.com/vercel/next.js/issues/43392 .

But after disabling one of them in development, the app ignore `dynamicParams = false` in the production mode. That means, even using `dynamicParams = false` with `generateStaticParams` to limit available params, the app would still accept dynamic params. In the MRE, even when `slug` of `/posts/[slug]` is limited to `1`, `/posts/2` still returns 200.

### Expected Behavior

`dynamicParams = false` works as expected, which means in the MRE `/posts/2` should return 404.

Firefox 108.0.2-1 in Arch Linux pacman source

title: _next/data/* URLs throwing 404, for multiple Next.js apps running on the same domain
body:
### Verify canary release

- [X] I verified that the issue exists in the latest Next.js canary release

### Provide environment information

```bash
Operating System:
      Platform: win32
      Arch: x64
      Version: Windows 10 Pro
    Binaries:
      Node: 18.12.0
      npm: N/A
      Yarn: N/A
      pnpm: N/A
    Relevant packages:
      next: 13.4.2
      eslint-config-next: 13.4.2
      react: 18.2.0
      react-dom: 18.2.0
      typescript: 5.0.4
```


### Which area(s) of Next.js are affected? (leave empty if unsure)

_No response_

### Link to the code that reproduces this issue

-

### To Reproduce

We still use the Routes Router approach in our apps, and the apps are containerised and running on the same domain, like:

domain: example.com

- app1 routes: 
  - /about
  - /contact

- app2 routes:
  - /shop
  - /basket

Under the same domain we have these URLs:
- example.com/about
- example.com/contact
- example.com/shop
- example.com/basket

Some of these pages are using SSR.

We set the `assetPrefix` for the static content in the `next.config`, so those easily can be mapped in Cloudflare and handled in the config with rewrite rule, like:
- app1:
  - `assetPrefix: 'app1/'`
  - rewrite rule in the config:
      ```
      {
          source: '/app1/:path*`,
          destination: '/:path*'
      }
      ```

  - Cloudflare:
     - request: example.com/about
     - fetch from: example.com/app1/about

- app2:
  - `assetPrefix: 'app2/'`
  - rewrite rule in the config:
      ```
      {
          source: '/app2/:path*`,
          destination: '/:path*'
      }
      ```

  - Cloudflare:
     - request: example.com/shop
     - fetch from: example.com/app2/shop



### Describe the Bug

In the browser, we have 404 errors like this:
- `https://example.com/next/data/<build-id>/about.json`
- `https://example.com/next/data/<build-id>/shop.json`

The issue is that there is no data fetch request path URL prefix option in the `next.config`. As a result, we cannot prefix the next server requests. Therefore, we cannot differentiate and map those requests to the right app/container.

### Expected Behavior

The Next.js should have a prefix option as it has for assets. It could be like `nextServerPrefix` or something like that, and it should be an empty string by default. Then we could set our prefix similarly to the `assetPrefix`, and the calls would look like these then:

- `https://example.com/app1/next/data/<build-id>/about.json`
- `https://example.com/app2/next/data/<build-id>/shop.json`

I found an article from 2021, so it looks like it is not a new issue, unfortunately. We have the same problem now as I described above:
https://arihantverma.com/posts/2021/07/18/workaround-nextjs-data-urls-same-domain-multiple-apps/

### Provide environment information

Operating System:

      react: 18.2.0
      react-dom: 18.2.0


### What browser are you using? (if relevant)

_No response_

### How are you deploying your application? (if relevant)

next start

### Describe the Bug

Deleted ISR page shows old generated document even though it is already revalidated and should show 404 page.
This usually works as expected, but if you disable ISR memory cache (by setting `isrMemoryCacheSize: 0`) it stops and always return the stale version of the page. 

### Expected Behavior

Deleted ISR page should return 404 page

### Link to reproduction

https://github.com/pekarja5/next-isr-cache

### To Reproduce

1. Build and start the production build of provided repo.
2. Navigate to `http://localhost:3000/detail/1`
3. In `public/detail.json` file, change the enabled parameter to 0
4. After 5 seconds (revalidation period) refresh the page twice. First refresh should serve you the stale page while revalidating the page on server and the second should return 404 page, but it does not. Any later request will still serve the original stale version of the page never reaching the expected 404 page.
